sglang.title: Maintain seq_lens_sum to make more FlashInfer operations non-blocking
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Maintain a seq_lens_sum on the CPU, so we do not need a blocking call on torch.cumsum for getting input indices for flashinfer.

sglang.title: Fix prefill oom
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR fixes #1739

Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.
 Add unit tests as outlined in the Contributor Guide.
 Update documentation as needed, including docstrings or example tutorials.

sglang.title: Faster overlap mode scheduler
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This PR improves the order of kernel launch and result fetching. Now the overlap scheduler can bring 10% throughput improvement even when radix cache is turned off. When the radix cache is turned on, we can expect more speedup.
Benchmark results
Overlap mode:  51.03 req/s
python -m sglang.launch_ser

sglang.title: misc: add CODEOWNERS
sglang.author: zhyncs
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.
 Add unit tests as outlined in the Contributor Guide.
 Update documentation as needed, including docstrings or example tutorials.

sglang.title: Fix sliding window attention and gemma-2 unit tests in CI
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Fix the wrong calculation for sliding window attention
Rename seq_lens_sum to paged_kernel_lens_sum in flashinfer_backend.py`
Monkey patch gemma2 in transformers to fix the OOM
Temporarily disable test_large_max_new_tokens.py in CI.

sglang.title: Llama3.2 vision model support
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation


Support encoder-decoder architecture in SGLang.
Support llama vision model.
Support CUDA graph and prefix cache for llama vision model

Note that to support CUDA graph for encoder-decoder architecture like llama vision (mllama), we should make encoder_lens the part of the cuda graph, as

sglang.title: Update `max_req_len` and `max_req_input_len`
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

More robustness, clarifying the prefill input ids truncation and max new tokens adjust.

Always leave 1 token for the max_req_len based on the max capacity.
Base on the max_req_len, leave 5 tokens for max_req_input_len
Leave 1 token for the adjusted max_new_tokens

Modifications

Checkli

vllm.title: [Doc] Consistent naming of attention backends
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Right now if you try to enable an unsupported feature (e.g., multi-step with xformers) you get a message like:
ValueError: Multi-Step not supported for attention backend: xformers. Set VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND to a value from ['flash-attn', 'rocm-flash-attn', 'flashinfer']

I find this confusing becau

vllm.title: [Model] FalconMamba Support
vllm.author: dhiaEddineRhaiem
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds the support for FalconMamba in VLLM.
In summary , FalconMamba is  the same Mamba class without the feed forward block and with the B_C_dt_rms LayerNorm.
Note that FalconMamba is now supported in transformers>=4.45.
I tried with this model https://huggingface.co/tiiuae/falcon-mamba-7b-in

This PR fixes a bug introduced by #8645.
related issues: #9448 & #9417 & #9409

This PR resolves the following issue encountered when running the ppc64le vLLM Docker container by ensuring it uses Python 3 from micromamba env, instead of the default system Python 3.
/usr/bin/python3: Error while finding module specification for 'vllm.entrypoints.o

vllm.title: [Model][Bugfix] Fix batching with multi-image in PixtralHF
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Before this PR, PixtralHF would fail during forward passes where pixel_values would be a list of lists of tensors. This can happen when multiple requests are batch that have multiple images each. Since each image can have a different shape, we end up with many separate tensors.
Due to how the HF pro

vllm.title: [Frontend] Reduce frequency of client cancellation checking
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The api server logic currently checks for client http connection disconnection for each request between every generated token. We can reduce the cost associated with this by limiting the checks to at most once per second.
With llama-3.2-1B, A100 tp=1, --num-scheduler-steps=8:



stream
multistep-str

vllm.title: [BugFix] Update draft model TP size check to allow matching target TP size
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We currently support draft spec decoding model TP size of either 1 or equal to the target model's TP size. The current check prevents the latter when the value is specified explicitly (though this is the default when not specified).
There is a stalled PR to allow other sizes #5856.

vllm.title: [Frontend] Don't log duplicate error stacktrace for every request in the batch
vllm.author: wallashss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Don't log duplicate error stacktrace for every request in the batch
EDIT: Discussed with @joerunde, and we changed the solution. On the client.py if the engine is errored, then the  server should shut down, to do that we have to make sure to send a MQEngineDeadError despite the type of the exception

vllm.title: :bug: Fixup more test failures from memory profiling
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This fixes a couple more tests that are now failing after followup fixes to cuda memory profiling in #9516
One lora test needed a bit more memory, and there seems to be a bug with some quantized models returning different results when run in different batches. This caused the score for the Meta-Llam

vllm.title: [core] move parallel sampling out from vllm core
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: try to hide seq group from the core, by handling parallel sampling in llm engine.

vllm.title: [Bugfix]: serialize config instances by value when using --trust-remote-code
vllm.author: tjohnson31415
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It is not currently possible to run vLLM with a model that requires --trust-remote-code if the server spans multiple nodes. The server will crash with an error when it attempts to communicate the dynamically generated configuration class to a remote Ray worker. The crux of the error is
ray.exception

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Remove unnecessary `fork_new_process`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In #8925, I accidentally copied over fork_new_process_for_each_test to the large_gpu_test when it's actually unnecessary (since current_platform.get_device_total_memory is supposed to be stateless).

vllm.author: Falko1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FILL IN THE PR DESCRIPTION HERE
FIX #9502
The proposed code fetches the values of rope_theta and max_position_embeddings from the model's config file. If they are not specified there, it will use the standard values 10000.0 and 2048, respectively.

This PR aims to replace some models on tests (Mostly Llama based) for smaller models when possible, for instance, I tried to replace most cases where meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-hf was used for meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B.
The goal is try to speed up the tests in general.
